event: diagnosed to have tropical chronic pancreatitis
presented to a medical center in India with similar pain
CT scan of the abdomen performed
plan to return for removal of ductal stones
hospitalized for pain control with narcotics
epigastric pain gradually increased in intensity
fatty atrophy of pancreatic head and uncinate process
pancreatic duct filled with large calcifications
possible obstruction at the level of the neck
admitted for management of pain secondary to chronic pancreatitis
transferred to the medical intensive care unit
activated recombinant human protein C started
major papilla of Vater expelling frank pus
fluid could not be aspirated through the cannula for culture
pancreatogram: marked dilatation of the main pancreatic duct
guide wire introduced into the pancreatic duct
approximately 20 ml of pus evacuated during duct manipulation
acute suppuration of the pancreatic duct (ASPD)
contrast enhanced CT scan of the abdomen performed
inflammatory changes within peripancreatic fat of body and tail consistent with edema
distal migration of calculus towards the sphincter of Oddi
Klebsiella ornithinolytica sensitive to the antibiotics given
transferred from the intensive care unit
